title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 45 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: National public holiday marking the start of the new year with celebrations across the country.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional de la Vendimia
festivals.description: Argentina's most famous grape harvest festival featuring parades, wine tastings, and the coronation of the harvest queen. A vibrant celebration of wine culture in the heart of wine country.

festivals.name: Carnival
festivals.description: Colorful street parades and celebrations throughout Argentina, with particularly vibrant displays in Buenos Aires, Corrientes, and Misiones. Features elaborate costumes, music, and dancing.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional de la Flor
festivals.description: A celebration of flowers and horticulture in Puerto Iguazú with exhibitions, parades, and cultural performances. Showcases the region's botanical richness and regional crafts.

festivals.name: Malvinas Day
festivals.description: National public holiday commemorating Argentina's claim to the Falkland Islands and those who died in the 1982 conflict. Marked by ceremonies and patriotic gatherings.

festivals.name: Semana Santa
festivals.description: Holy Week celebrated throughout Argentina with religious processions, church services, and cultural events. A deeply significant Christian observance with regional variations.

festivals.name: Labour Day
festivals.description: National public holiday celebrating workers' rights with parades and demonstrations across Argentina.

festivals.name: National Day of Memory for Truth and Justice
festivals.description: National public holiday remembering victims of the military dictatorship with commemorative ceremonies and marches throughout the country.

festivals.name: Fiesta de Corpus Christi
festivals.description: Religious celebration in northwest Argentina featuring processions and traditional indigenous-Catholic fusion ceremonies. Particularly vibrant in Jujuy and Salta provinces.

festivals.name: Winter Festival
festivals.description: Celebration of the winter solstice in Ushuaia with outdoor activities, concerts, and cultural events showcasing Patagonian culture and traditions.

festivals.name: Fiesta de los Reyes Magos
festivals.description: Religious celebration of Epiphany with traditional parades and cultural events throughout Argentina, particularly significant in Santiago del Estero.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional del Poncho
festivals.description: Festival celebrating traditional Argentine ponchos and gaucho culture with artisan exhibitions, folk music, and horse shows. A showcase of regional handicrafts and heritage.

festivals.name: Independence Day
festivals.description: National public holiday commemorating Argentina's declaration of independence from Spain in 1816 with parades, ceremonies, and celebrations nationwide.

festivals.name: Fiesta de las Flores
festivals.description: Spring flower festival in Concordia celebrating the region's agricultural abundance with floral displays, parades, and cultural performances.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional de la Frontera
festivals.description: Festival celebrating the tri-border region of Argentina, Paraguay, and Brazil with cultural exchanges, music, food, and regional traditions.

festivals.name: Tango Festival
festivals.description: International celebration of tango with performances, competitions, and master classes showcasing this iconic Argentine cultural form. A must-see for tango enthusiasts worldwide.

festivals.name: Festival Nacional de la Chaya
festivals.description: Pre-Carnival festival in the northwest featuring water-throwing celebrations, music, and dancing with deep indigenous roots and regional flavor.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Canción
festivals.description: Song festival celebrating Argentine folk music and traditional melodies with performances by renowned musicians and regional artists.

festivals.name: Fiesta Provincial del Turismo
festivals.description: Summer tourism festival in Patagonia featuring water sports, adventure activities, and cultural celebrations showcasing the region's outdoor attractions.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Tradición
festivals.description: Celebration of gaucho heritage and rural traditions with horse shows, folk music, traditional food, and artisan displays honoring Argentina's cowboy culture.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional del Ternero
festivals.description: Festival celebrating beef and cattle ranching with traditional barbecues, livestock competitions, and gaucho demonstrations in central Argentina.

festivals.name: Peña de Morón
festivals.description: December folk music festival in Buenos Aires suburbs featuring live performances of traditional Argentine folk music and cultural activities.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Cerveza
festivals.description: Beer festival in Welsh Patagonia celebrating regional breweries and Germanic heritage with tastings, food, and cultural performances.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Mazorca
festivals.description: Corn festival celebrating the harvest season with traditional food, crafts, music, and agricultural exhibitions in the Mesopotamia region.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional del Inmigrante
festivals.description: Festival celebrating immigration heritage in Oberá with cultural presentations, traditional foods, and crafts from various immigrant communities.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional de la Empanada
festivals.description: Festival dedicated to Argentina's beloved empanada pastry with cooking competitions, tastings, and celebrations of regional culinary traditions in the northwest.

festivals.name: Fiesta del Libro de Buenos Aires
festivals.description: International book fair attracting publishers and readers from around the world, celebrating literature and Argentina's rich literary tradition.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Pachamama
festivals.description: Indigenous celebration honoring Mother Earth with traditional rituals, food offerings, and spiritual ceremonies rooted in Andean beliefs across the northwest.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Primavera
festivals.description: Spring celebration nationwide marked by parades, cultural events, and the famous flower parade in Buenos Aires celebrating the arrival of spring.

festivals.name: Death of General José de San Martín
festivals.description: National public holiday honoring the memory of Argentina's independence hero with commemorative ceremonies and educational events.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional del Turismo de Aventura
festivals.description: Adventure tourism festival showcasing Mendoza's outdoor activities including rock climbing, white-water rafting, and mountain expeditions.

festivals.name: Fiesta de los Andes
festivals.description: Festival celebrating Andean culture and traditions with indigenous music, crafts, and spiritual ceremonies in Argentina's mountain region.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional de Doma y Folklore
festivals.description: Rural festival celebrating horsemanship and folklore with traditional gaucho demonstrations, folk music, and artisan exhibitions in the northwest.

festivals.name: Fiesta del Chocolate
festivals.description: Chocolate festival in Bariloche celebrating the region's famous chocolate industry with tastings, craft exhibitions, and food demonstrations.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional de los Frutos de Estación
festivals.description: Festival celebrating seasonal fruits and agricultural harvest in the subtropical north with food exhibitions, cultural performances, and traditional crafts.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Yerba Mate
festivals.description: Festival honoring Argentina's traditional yerba mate beverage with tastings, cultural exhibitions, and celebrations of this iconic regional drink in Oberá.

festivals.name: National Sovereignty Day
festivals.description: National public holiday commemorating Argentina's national sovereignty and founding with official ceremonies and civic celebrations.

festivals.name: Christmas
festivals.description: National public holiday celebrated throughout Argentina with religious services, family gatherings, and festive celebrations marking the birth of Christ.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional de la Nata
festivals.description: Cream and dairy festival in Villa La Angostura celebrating the region's dairy traditions with cheese tastings, food, and artisan demonstrations.

festivals.name: Fiesta del Gaucho
festivals.description: Celebration of gaucho culture and horsemanship in the pampas region with traditional rodeos, folk music, and displays of rural heritage.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional de la Uva
festivals.description: Grape festival in Argentina's second wine-producing region featuring wine tastings, parades, and celebrations of the harvest season in San Juan.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Diversidad Sexual
festivals.description: LGBTQ+ pride celebration in Buenos Aires with vibrant parades, performances, and cultural events celebrating diversity and inclusion.

festivals.name: Fiesta Nacional del Choclo
festivals.description: Festival celebrating corn and regional cuisine in central Argentina with cooking competitions, traditional food, and agricultural exhibitions.

festivals.name: Fiesta de la Cosecha
festivals.description: Harvest festival in the subtropical northeast celebrating agricultural abundance with regional crafts, music, and traditional foods.

festivals.name: Festival de Cine de Mar del Plata
festivals.description: International film festival showcasing cinema from around the world with screenings, premieres, and cultural discussions in the Atlantic coastal city.
